title: Authors and Citation
text: Wei Hong. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hong W (2026). OncReg: Cancer Registry Tools. R package version 0.17.5, https://github.com/hongconsulting/OncReg.
code: @Manual{,   title = {OncReg: Cancer Registry Tools},   author = {Wei Hong},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.17.5},   url = {https://github.com/hongconsulting/OncReg}, }

title: Cancer Registry Tools
text: Tools working cancer registry data.

title: Cancer Registry Tools
text: can install development version OncReg GitHub :

what: Example: comma-separated free text, Microsoft Excel dates, and survival analysis
title: Cancer Registry Tools
code: library(OncReg) options(width = 120) diagnosis <- c("01/01/00", "01/01/2000", "36526", "36526", "36526") treatment <- c("capecitabine",                 "LETROZOLE",                 "letrozole, palbociclib",                 "Letrozole,Ribociclib",                 "anastrozole, ribociclib") progression <- c("01/01/2001", ".", "01/01/2001", "", NA) review1 <- c("01/07/2001", "01/07/2001", "01/07/2001", "01/07/2001", "01/07/2001") review2 <- c(NA, NA, NA, NA, "01/01/2002") death <- c("01/01/2002", "01/01/2002", NA, NA, NA) data0 <- data.frame("id" = 1:5, "diagnosis" = diagnosis, "treatment" = treatment, "progression" = progression, "review1" = review1, "review2" = review2, "death" = death) print(data0) #>   id  diagnosis               treatment progression    review1    review2      death #> 1  1   01/01/00            capecitabine  01/01/2001 01/07/2001       <NA> 01/01/2002 #> 2  2 01/01/2000               LETROZOLE           . 01/07/2001       <NA> 01/01/2002 #> 3  3      36526  letrozole, palbociclib  01/01/2001 01/07/2001       <NA>       <NA> #> 4  4      36526    Letrozole,Ribociclib             01/07/2001       <NA>       <NA> #> 5  5      36526 anastrozole, ribociclib        <NA> 01/07/2001 01/01/2002       <NA>  # delimited strings data1 <- data.frame("id" = data0$id, "treatment" = data0$treatment) data1$treatment <- OR.delim.replace(data1$treatment, "anastrozole", "ai") data1$treatment <- OR.delim.replace(data1$treatment, "letrozole", "ai") data1$treatment <- OR.delim.replace(data1$treatment, "palbociclib", "cdk46i") data1$treatment <- OR.delim.replace(data1$treatment, "ribociclib", "cdk46i")  # Microsoft Excel dates data1$diagnosis <- OR.dmyY.to.Excel(data0$diagnosis, 20, 25) data1$progression <- OR.dmyY.to.Excel(data0$progression, 20, 25) data1$review1 <- OR.dmyY.to.Excel(data0$review1, 20, 25) data1$review2 <- OR.dmyY.to.Excel(data0$review2, 20, 25) data1$death <- OR.dmyY.to.Excel(data0$death, 20, 25)  # progression-free survival and overall survival: # - PFS date is the date of progression or death, whichever is earlier data1$PFS <- OR.rowmin(cbind(data1$progression, data1$death))  # - last review date is the latest date patient was observed alive data1$lastreview <- OR.rowmax(cbind(data1$review1, data1$review2)) data1$PFSmonths <- OR.survoutcome(data1$diagnosis, data1$PFS, data1$lastreview)[, 1] data1$PFSstatus <- OR.survoutcome(data1$diagnosis, data1$PFS, data1$lastreview)[, 2] data1$OSmonths <- OR.survoutcome(data1$diagnosis, data1$death, data1$lastreview)[, 1] data1$OSstatus <- OR.survoutcome(data1$diagnosis, data1$death, data1$lastreview)[, 2]  print(data1) #>   id    treatment diagnosis progression review1 review2 death   PFS lastreview PFSmonths PFSstatus OSmonths OSstatus #> 1  1 capecitabine     36526       36892   37073      NA 37257 36892      37073  12.02489         1 24.01692        1 #> 2  2           ai     36526          NA   37073      NA 37257 37257      37073  24.01692         1 24.01692        1 #> 3  3   ai, cdk46i     36526       36892   37073      NA    NA 36892      37073  12.02489         1 17.97162        0 #> 4  4   ai, cdk46i     36526          NA   37073      NA    NA    NA      37073  17.97162         0 17.97162        0 #> 5  5   ai, cdk46i     36526          NA   37073   37257    NA    NA      37257  24.01692         0 24.01692        0

title: Cancer Registry Tools

what: Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber
title: Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber
text: Computes Ronchetti's robust analogue¹ Akaike information criterion² (AICR) linear model fitted Huber M-estimation³. effective degrees freedom term evaluated using Moore–Penrose pseudoinverse⁴ numerical stability rank deficiency.

title: Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber
Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber
text: X numeric design matrix. y numeric response vector. beta numeric vector regression coefficients Huber M-estimation fit. scale scale estimate Huber M-estimation fit. k2 tuning constant Huber ψ-function. Default = 1.345.

title: Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber
text: AICR value.

title: Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber
text: Ronchetti, E., 1985. Robust model selection regression. Statistics & Probability Letters, 3(1), pp. 21–23. Akaike, H., 1974. new look statistical model identification. IEEE Transactions Automatic Control, 19(6), pp. 716–723. Huber, P.J., 1973. Robust regression: asymptotics, conjectures Monte Carlo. Annals Statistics, pp. 799–821. Penrose, R., 1955. generalized inverse matrices. : Mathematical Proceedings Cambridge Philosophical Society, 51(3), pp. 406–413. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press.

title: Robust Akaike information criterion for Huber M-estimation — AICR.Huber

what: Travelling salesman distance via Google Routes API — OR.Google.TSP
title: Travelling salesman distance via Google Routes API — OR.Google.TSP
text: Computes shortest round-trip distance km starting ending origin, visiting destination exactly , via brute-force enumeration permutations. Distances computed via .Google.distance().

title: Travelling salesman distance via Google Routes API — OR.Google.TSP
Travelling salesman distance via Google Routes API — OR.Google.TSP
text: origin String starting ending address. destinations String vector addresses visit. key String Google API key. echo Boolean indicating whether print progress summary console. Default = FALSE.

title: Travelling salesman distance via Google Routes API — OR.Google.TSP
text: Numeric scalar shortest round-trip distance km.

what: Resolve address using Google Geocoding API — OR.Google.address
title: Resolve address using Google Geocoding API — OR.Google.address
text: Returns formatted address given input string. reduce API calls, addresses case-insensitive whitespace-trimmed, empty addresses processing return NA early, results memoized.

title: Resolve address using Google Geocoding API — OR.Google.address
Resolve address using Google Geocoding API — OR.Google.address
text: address String input address. key String Google API key.

title: Road distance between two addresses using Google Routes API — OR.Google.distance
text: Returns road distance km two addresses. reduce API calls, addresses case-insensitive whitespace-trimmed, empty addresses processing return NA early, processed addresses internally sorted, results memoized.

title: Road distance between two addresses using Google Routes API — OR.Google.distance
Road distance between two addresses using Google Routes API — OR.Google.distance
text: String address . b String address B. key String Google API key.

title: Road distance between two addresses using Google Routes API — OR.Google.distance

what: Convert strings to numeric after normalising minus signs and stripping digit grouping separators — OR.as.numeric
title: Convert strings to numeric after normalising minus signs and stripping digit grouping separators — OR.as.numeric
text: Replaces Unicode minus signs ("\u2212") ASCII "-", removes either commas spaces used digit grouping (thousands) separators, converts result numeric. Non-convertible values return NA warnings suppressed.

what: Collapse repeated measurements across IDs — OR.collapse
title: Collapse repeated measurements across IDs — OR.collapse
text: ID data frame, collapses rows belonging ID single row. Variables whose names match specified pattern summarized according chosen method. output data frame contains one row per unique ID one column per matched variable. Non-matching variables ignored.

title: Collapse repeated measurements across IDs — OR.collapse
Collapse repeated measurements across IDs — OR.collapse
text: data data frame containing repeated measurements. ID_varname Character string giving name ID variable. Rows ID treated belonging individual. pattern String containing regular expression used select variables. variable names matching pattern (via grep()) collapsed. method Character string specifying multiple values within ID collapsed. Options include: "left": Returns first non-missing value order appearance. "mean": Returns mean non-missing values. "max": Returns maximum non-missing values. "min": Returns minimum non-missing values. "single" (default): Returns mean non-missing values prints message one unique non-missing value found. "sum": Returns sum non-missing values.

title: Collapse repeated measurements across IDs — OR.collapse
text: data frame one row per unique ID one column variable matching pattern, summarized according method.

what: #' Detect if all substrings are contained in each string vector element #' (case-insensitive) #' #' Checks whether each element of a string vector contains all of the specified #' substrings using case-insensitive matching. #' @param x String vector. #' @param match String vector of substrings to look for within each element of #' x. #' @return Logical vector indicating whether each element contains a match. #' @export OR.contains.AND <- function(x, match) f <- function(x) g <- function(match) return(grepl(tolower(match), tolower(x), fixed = TRUE)) return(all(sapply(match, g))) return(unname(sapply(x, f))) — OR.dmy.to.dmY
title: #' Detect if all substrings are contained in each string vector element #' (case-insensitive) #' #' Checks whether each element of a string vector contains all of the specified #' substrings using case-insensitive matching. #' @param x String vector. #' @param match String vector of substrings to look for within each element of #' x. #' @return Logical vector indicating whether each element contains a match. #' @export OR.contains.AND <- function(x, match) f <- function(x) g <- function(match) return(grepl(tolower(match), tolower(x), fixed = TRUE)) return(all(sapply(match, g))) return(unname(sapply(x, f))) — OR.dmy.to.dmY
text: #' Detect substrings contained string vector element #' (case-insensitive) #' #' Checks whether element string vector contains specified #' substrings using case-insensitive matching. #' @param x String vector. #' @param match String vector substrings look within element #' x. #' @return Logical vector indicating whether element contains match. #' @export .contains.<- function(x, match) f <- function(x) g <- function(match) return(grepl(tolower(match), tolower(x), fixed = TRUE))return((sapply(match, g)))return(unname(sapply(x, f)))

title: #' Detect if all substrings are contained in each string vector element #' (case-insensitive) #' #' Checks whether each element of a string vector contains all of the specified #' substrings using case-insensitive matching. #' @param x String vector. #' @param match String vector of substrings to look for within each element of #' x. #' @return Logical vector indicating whether each element contains a match. #' @export OR.contains.AND <- function(x, match) f <- function(x) g <- function(match) return(grepl(tolower(match), tolower(x), fixed = TRUE)) return(all(sapply(match, g))) return(unname(sapply(x, f))) — OR.dmy.to.dmY

title: Polynomial linear non-parametric outlier plot — OR.outliers.rlm.ggplot

title: Polynomial linear non-parametric outlier plot — OR.outliers.rlm.ggplot
code: OR.outliers.rlm.ggplot(   x,   y,   max.degree = 3,   p = 0.05,   tol.min = 1e-04,   tol.target = 1e-04,   col.in = "#0072B5FF",   col.out = "#BC3C29FF",   echo = FALSE,   x.breaks = NA,   x.labels = NA,   x.title = "",   y.breaks = NA,   y.title = "" )

title: Polynomial linear non-parametric outlier plot — OR.outliers.rlm.ggplot
text: x Numeric predictor vector. y Numeric response vector. max.degree Maximum polynomial degree considered. Default = 3. Internally capped n - 2, n number non-missing observations. p Target two-sided exclusion proportion normality residual-based modified z-score rule. Default = 0.05. tol.min M-estimation minimum convergence tolerance. Default = 0.0001. tol.target M-estimation target convergence tolerance. Default = 0.0001. col.Color used fitted curve, ribbon band, observations flagged outliers. Default = "#0072B5FF". col.Color used observations flagged outliers. Default = "#BC3C29FF". echo Logical. TRUE, prints internal data frame used plot together computed k MAD values. x.breaks Numeric vector specifying x-axis tick locations. NA, values x used. x.labels Labels x-axis ticks. NA, values x used. x.title Title x-axis. y.breaks Numeric vector specifying y-axis tick locations. Horizontal gridlines drawn values. y.title Title y-axis.

title: Polynomial linear non-parametric outlier plot — OR.outliers.rlm.ggplot

title: Sum with NA removal or NA if no valid entries — OR.sum
Sum with NA removal or NA if no valid entries — OR.sum
text: x Numeric vector.

title: Sum with NA removal or NA if no valid entries — OR.sum
text: Sum non-NA elements NA none exist.

title: Survival outcomes — OR.survoutcome
text: Computes event times statuses start, event, review dates.

title: Survival outcomes — OR.survoutcome
Survival outcomes — OR.survoutcome
text: date_start Vector start dates. date_event Vector event dates. date_follow Vector last follow-dates censored cases. divisor Unit conversion factor time. Default = 365.2425/12.

title: Survival outcomes — OR.survoutcome
text: numeric matrix two columns: survival time status (1 = event, 0 = censored). Cases missing start dates survival time ≤ 0 returned NA columns.

what: Identify treatment restart after a minimum treatment-free interval — OR.txlines.restart
title: Identify treatment restart after a minimum treatment-free interval — OR.txlines.restart
text: patient (rows), scans treatment lines (columns) identify earliest line whose start date occurs least t_min time units recent prior stop date. Also computes cumulative treatment duration restart, maximum stop reason restart (example, 0 = stopped due toxicity, 1 = stopped due progressive disease), treatment-free interval, earliest progression date restart.

title: Identify treatment restart after a minimum treatment-free interval — OR.txlines.restart
code: OR.txlines.restart(   date_start,   date_stop,   reason_stop,   date_prog,   t_min = 182.62125 )

title: Identify treatment restart after a minimum treatment-free interval — OR.txlines.restart
text: date_start Numeric matrix treatment start dates. date_stop Numeric matrix treatment stop dates. reason_stop Numeric matrix encoding reason treatment stopped. date_prog Numeric matrix progression dates. t_min Minimum treatment-free interval required qualify restart. Default = 365.2425/2.

title: Identify treatment restart after a minimum treatment-free interval — OR.txlines.restart
text: Data frame one row per patient six columns: prev_time Cumulative treatment duration restart. stop_reason Maximum stopping reason code observed restart. interval_time Interval latest stop restart. restart_line Index treatment line restart occurs. restart_date Start date restart line. reprog_date Earliest progression date occurring restart. restart satisfying t_min found, interval_time, restart_line, restart_date, reprog_date returned NA, prev_time stop_reason summarize full observed treatment history.

title: Construct treatment timelines (long format) — OR.txlines.timeline
text: id, takes treatment lines stored wide format (one column per line treatment treatment regimens, start dates, stop dates, stop reasons, event dates) constructs time-ordered timeline long format. output row corresponds unique date treatment starts stops, event (example, disease progression) occurs. unique date, active treatment regimens stop reasons merged.

title: Construct treatment timelines (long format) — OR.txlines.timeline
code: OR.txlines.timeline(   id,   regimens,   startdates,   stopdates,   stopreasons = NULL,   eventdates = NULL,   echo = TRUE )

title: Construct treatment timelines (long format) — OR.txlines.timeline
text: id Vector identifiers. regimens Comma-delimited string matrix data frame treatment regimens (one row per id, one column per line treatment). startdates Numeric matrix data frame start dates (one row per id, one column per line treatment). stopdates Numeric matrix data frame stop dates (one row per id, one column per line treatment). stopreasons Optional comma-delimited string matrix data frame stop reasons (one row per id, one column per line treatment). eventdates Optional numeric matrix data frame event dates (one row per id, one column per line treatment). echo Logical. TRUE, prints additional information console. Default = TRUE.

title: Construct treatment timelines (long format) — OR.txlines.timeline
text: data frame long format columns: id Identifier. date Date treatment starts stops, event occurs. regimen Active regimens date. stopreasons Stop reasons occurring date. event Logical indicating whether event occurs date.

what: Truncate stop dates by maximum duration — OR.txlines.truncate
title: Truncate stop dates by maximum duration — OR.txlines.truncate
text: Limits duration date_start date_stop t_max truncating date_stop necessary.

title: Truncate stop dates by maximum duration — OR.txlines.truncate
Truncate stop dates by maximum duration — OR.txlines.truncate
text: date_start Numeric matrix start dates. date_stop Numeric matrix stop dates. t_max Maximum allowed duration.

title: Truncate stop dates by maximum duration — OR.txlines.truncate
text: Numeric matrix truncated stop dates date_stop - date_start exceed t_max.

what: Convert treatment timelines to wide format — OR.txlines.wide
title: Convert treatment timelines to wide format — OR.txlines.wide
text: Takes treatment timelines long format (produced .txlines.timeline()) converts wide format one row per id one set start date, regimen, event date columns per treatment line.

title: Convert treatment timelines to wide format — OR.txlines.wide
Convert treatment timelines to wide format — OR.txlines.wide
text: id Vector identifiers. date Numeric vector dates (sorted within id). regimen Comma-delimited string vector active regimens date. event Logical vector indicating whether event occurs date. line Numeric vector indicating treatment line date.

title: Convert treatment timelines to wide format — OR.txlines.wide
text: data frame wide format columns, k indexes treatment lines: id Identifier. date.startL Start date line k. regimenL Regimens line k. date.eventL Earliest event date line k.

what: Robust linear regression using Huber M-estimation — rlm.Huber
title: Robust linear regression using Huber M-estimation — rlm.Huber
text: Fits linear regression model using Huber M-estimation¹ via iteratively reweighted least squares.

title: Robust linear regression using Huber M-estimation — rlm.Huber
code: rlm.Huber(X, y, k2 = 1.345, maxit = 100, tol.min = 1e-04, tol.target = 1e-04)

title: Robust linear regression using Huber M-estimation — rlm.Huber
text: X numeric design matrix. y numeric response vector. k2 tuning constant Huber ψ-function. Default = 1.345. maxit maximum number iterations. Default = 100. tol.min minimum convergence tolerance. Default = 0.0001. tol.target target convergence tolerance. Default = 0.0001.

title: Robust linear regression using Huber M-estimation — rlm.Huber
text: object classes "lm" "rlm", broadly compatible objects returned MASS::rlm() minor differences due rounding.

title: Robust linear regression using Huber M-estimation — rlm.Huber
text: Huber, P.J., 1973. Robust regression: asymptotics, conjectures Monte Carlo. Annals Statistics, pp. 799–821.
